prompt: Tambah edit image bukti pengambilan di backoffice halaman delivery detail. Fitur crop + rotate.
expected_avoid: TypeScript syntax (.tsx, interface, type annotations) — backoffice is JS
writing edited image straight over the original storage path (loses audit trail)
expected_use: native Canvas API (HTMLCanvasElement + getContext('2d')) for crop + rotate — hand-rolled, no third-party image lib
useState for crop rect + rotation angle + dirty flag
MUI Modal OR @dash/modal as the editor surface (backoffice tolerates either; stay consistent with the surrounding page)
axios POST to nodejs-core / ts-delivery proof-update endpoint with NextAuth bearer
Two storage paths: original retained at proof-original/<id>, edited written to proof-edited/<id>
Audit trail log: log original URL + edited URL + editor (session.user.id) + reason + timestamp on every save
expected_pattern_ref: External-lib policy — refuse anti-pattern catalog 'fresh image editor lib' (Phase 0.1 addition); Audit trail rule — every edit on user-uploaded proof MUST persist {original_url, edited_url, editor_id, reason, ts} (Phase 0.2 addition); Adaptation Layer — next-backoffice-web (rules L388-396); Anti-Pattern Catalog 'Custom Modal' caveat (rules L336); Refuse anti-pattern #1 RHF (rules L577)
context_notes: Backoffice = JS Pages Router, NextAuth, three-UI-lib coexistence (MUI + antd + Tailwind). MUST log original + edited URL + editor + reason. Original image must NEVER be overwritten — it is regulatory evidence (POD/POP). Canvas-API hand-roll is the policy because the external image-editor libs (react-easy-crop, cropperjs, react-image-crop) each pull 30-80KB + their own state model that fights useState; the crop+rotate scope is small enough for ~80 lines of Canvas code. Editor UX should match surrounding modal (MUI Dialog if page already uses MUI, @dash/modal greenfield).
